title: Absolute dogwater of an app
review:
EDIT:
Updating this review because my experience has only gotten worse. My account was placed on a block because my spouse tried adding her card to Apple Wallet. I spent 30 minutes waiting on the phone and being constantly redirected across departments to finally get the block lifted. Attempted a transaction and was put on a block again. Bank of America’s fraud system is so bad and prone for false positives that it’s like they don’t want me as a customer or something. They are making it extremely hard to find reasons to want to stay as a customer. I’m very frustrated and it’s only been 1 month into using this card.

ORIGINAL REVIEW:
App is SOOO annoyingly cluttered. Every single time I open the app I get some or other pop-up about an annoying disclaimer, promotion, or confirmation of contact information. It’s like their team designed the app just to make the legal team happy instead of making it for users trying to do simple everyday financial tasks in an easy and quick way.

Also trying to connect an account via their direct account connection straight up does not work.

I’m only using this app because they’re the bank provider for my airline card. But based on the app, I would not consider banking with them. I’m also a Citibank and Chase user and, among the big banks, Chase has the best app by far.

I don’t understand how they have such a bad app in this day and age when well designed apps are so common. Meanwhile, fintechs are gaining market share because they actually understand how important this is.

title: Mobile Banking on BofA
review:
I have been a customer since 1996 since I moved to Charlotte, NC.
I have been a very satisfied customer. More than satisfied! BofA has great fraud protection and always checks to make sure my transactions are legitimate if they see anything suspicious. We had a home equity loan before we paid our home off and were very happy with the interest rates and customer service. I once was scammed through Zelle and BofA refunded the entire amount that was stolen by these horrible scam professionals. They have always been there to help me with life’s financial situations and I would recommend anyone who is interested in finding a new bank. My only suggestion is they offer more ATM’s in the area I currently moved, 22 miles outside of Charlotte in Sherrills Ford. There is not one BofA ATM within 7 miles from this area which is highly disappointing because I have to use cash ATM’s that charge me a fee and BofA also charges their fee. Maybe BofA would consider not charging their loyal customers a fee since we don’t have a BofA atm locally or a Bank of America to use. I’m sure a lot of your customers would appreciate not being charged twice to get their own money!  Otherwise I would give a 5 star review. I need to change it to 3 stars for this inconvenience and chargers!

Not everybody can afford new devices all the time like your devs and execs who made this dumb decision. This directly inflicts annoyance and harm to people of lower income. This is a terrible way to treat your long term customers, and I will be closing my account and moving banks after 17 years with you unless this is resolved by next week or so when I have a chance to get there and deal with all of that. Good job 🔩-ing over your long term customers. Hopefully somebody will care and fix this, but I doubt that’ll be the case. I’ve had no luck or help solving other issues in the past. Upon launch, at the bottom, it says “what would you like the power to do?” Well, I’d like the power to use my dang app again, and actually manage MY account and MY money on MY phone, and if my device isn’t updated to your liking, that’s MY CHOICE.

title: Terrible App Design, but the Bank Itself Is Good
review:
The Bank of America app has one of the worst user experience designs I’ve seen, and it has stayed that way for years without any real fixes.

Every time you try to send money, you have to remember and type the exact amount because the app doesn’t show your statement balance, minimum balance, or total available balance. You’re forced to switch between screens and keep the number in your head.

Entered amount but after changed destination? Enter it again because the app erases it.

You can’t copy any numbers anywhere. I just want to view a transaction and copy its amount if needed, but the app doesn’t allow that. So I have to remember another number here.

Copied somewhere else, going to paste, no way! Not all fields work to paste from clipboard, you have to type manually. So many mistakes done because of the pure UX.

The whole app feels like a web page pretending to be an app. It’s not a real native iOS experience, just a slow web wrapper that looks and feels outdated.

To be fair, the bank’s service itself is good. The app is what ruins the experience.
If they ever fix the app properly, I’ll come back and update this review.

The last (?) update also took away some functionality, and the bill pay has become more complex. Bill pay is one by one and multiple clicks to get there.   I tried to do 2 things today - pay my credit card bill and use reward points. Under amount, you used to be able to click on a few options, incl “pay statement balance”. Now you have to type in the amount, which of course I fat fingered and then couldn’t change, so had to make a 2nd payment. And then I couldn’t complete the rewards transfer. You need to swipe at one point and it just doesn’t work. I had to go to a computer. I don’t do anything too complicated on the app, I just want it to work when I need it to and not have to jump through hoops once I’m in.
